behavior_surfaces: workflow template phase schema now supports explicit per-phase toolPolicy.allowedTools
workflow template validation normalizes and rejects invalid or unknown per-phase tool policy values
workflow runtime phase tool gating uses explicit allowedTools when present and preserves built-in defaults when absent
workflow built-in tools exposed to agents, including submit_phase_completion and workflow_template_authoring, can be allowed or denied per phase
in-process workflow tool pool assembly filters both base tools and workflow-scoped tools by active phase policy
WebSocket workflow session startup passes custom phase policy through CLI disallowedTools
desktop workflow template editor exposes runtime and workflow built-in tool access controls per phase
desktop workflow editor warns when submit_phase_completion is disabled and avoids writing default toolPolicy into legacy templates unless the policy is user-edited or already exists
state_contracts: Templates without toolPolicy retain existing phase-derived tool defaults.
Templates with explicit toolPolicy compute disallowedTools from the known configurable tool list.
Unknown tool names in toolPolicy are rejected at template validation.
submit_phase_completion is no longer unconditionally exposed when explicit phase tool policy omits it.
workflow_template_authoring operations are denied when the active explicit phase tool policy omits workflow_template_authoring.
Existing already-running agent sessions keep their already-launched tool set; the new policy applies on restart, retry, or a new session launch.
Desktop saves root-level phase.toolPolicy and contract.toolPolicy for custom policies while preserving old templates without an explicit policy.

confidence_notes: Server tests prove explicit phase tool policy overrides default restrictions and controls workflow scoped tools.
Template validation tests prove policies round-trip from both flat and grouped phase shapes and reject unknown tools.
WebSocket test proves launch options reflect custom allowedTools by denying all other managed and workflow built-in tools.
Desktop test proves Settings editor can view, edit, warn, and persist per-phase tool choices.
Compatibility-preserving editor save logic avoids adding default toolPolicy to templates that did not have an explicit policy.
user_decisions: Expose workflow phase tool access in Settings workflow editor.
Let users decide which tools each phase can use.
Include workflow built-in tools exposed to agents in the same configurable surface.
Keep first implementation bounded to known managed tools and known workflow built-in tools.

included: Known managed runtime tools: Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit, Bash, PowerShell, Agent
Known workflow built-in tools: workflow_template_authoring and submit_phase_completion
